verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: Boundary and rationale documented; Isolation vs survival separated as orthogonal; Referenced by [[tasks/yzx-iso/t7-0-lane-index]]; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gates committed red at lifeos a82c166 (3/3 failing: spec absent), green at HEAD (3/3)
gate_result.boundary_evidence: Spec section 3 records the one honest limit without euphemism: the kernel is shared (the source of zero hypervisor latency, I03/I04), so a host kernel upgrade plus reboot ends LifeOS processes but never touches LifeOS state (I01/I05).
gate_result.orthogonality_evidence: Isolation (namespaces/envelope/control plane, protecting the siblings while both run) and survival (durable tier + re-attach across inevitable reboots) are stated as orthogonal concerns, with Survival = durable tier + re-attach as the normative formula (I10).
gate_result.reference_evidence: Section 3 is referenced by tasks/yzx-iso/t7-0-lane-index (boot re-attach, ARCHBP-093..100), and the lane index carries the spec backlink in GitKB.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: tests/archbp-060-kernel-boundary.spec.ts validates the published spec's boundary statements, the orthogonality separation, and the survival formula.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-060 is complete: the shared-kernel boundary is documented as spec section 3 — namespaces share the host kernel (the source of zero latency), a reboot ends LifeOS processes but never LifeOS state, and isolation vs survival are separated as orthogonal concerns with Survival = durable tier + re-attach. The section is the t7 boot re-attach lane's stated authority.
